seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Comboyne and Comerong Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Comboyne vs Comerong Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Comboyne and Comerong Island.
heading: Comboyne vs Comerong Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Comboyne, New South Wales and Comerong Island, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Comboyne has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Comerong Island (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Comboyne or Comerong Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Comboyne has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Comerong Island scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Comboyne scores 32/100 for bushfire risk. Comerong Island scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Comboyne or Comerong Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Comboyne has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
